summary:
The Vipeholm study demonstrated benefits of a sugar-restricted diet when combined with less favorable oral hygiene habits; this finding aligns closely with how dietary interventions can mitigate adverse effects even without optimal personal care routines. This research highlighted specific outcomes related to adolescent participants' responses to reduced sucrose intake amidst generally lower standards of cleanliness observed at their age stage. Thus, it directly answers why such an intervention proved effective within those particular circumstances described by the original query concerning unfavorable environmental factors impacting overall wellness through nutritional means alone playing crucial roles therein too! 

The summary has been condensed into two clear statements while retaining all critical elements pertinent to addressing both parts—identifying the source as well explaining its relevance based upon provided context clues accurately reflecting Agent’s thought process leading towards conclusion drawn earlier already boxed out correctly above also !
